input: INDICATION: Trauma status post fall, on Coumadin and seen in back in chest pain. COMPARISON: CT abdomen and pelvis from _ _ _. TECHNIQUE: Helical CT acquisitions from the lung bases through the pubic symphysis after administration of intravenous contrast. Multiplanar reformations obtained. CT OF THE CHEST WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST: There is asymmetric pulmonary edema predominantly worse on the right side with ground-glass opacity, interlobular septal thickening and small right pleural effusion. There is bibasilar atelectasis. Several scattered pulmonary nodules are present, the largest in the left lower lobe measuring 1.5 cm. Linear scarring is noted at the left lung base. There is tracheobronchial tree calcification. There are multiple enlarged lymph nodes, the largest in the precarinal station measures 1.5 x 1.9 cm. Other small subcentimeter nodes are noted in the upper paratracheal stations. Atherosclerotic aortic calcifications are present. The heart is enlarged with particularly right atrial enlargement. There is no pericardial effusion. CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST: The liver, gallbladder, adrenals, spleen, abdominal bowel loops are unremarkable. The kidney demonstrates a large simple cyst in the inferior pole, measuring 4.7 x 5.2 cm, unchanged. The left kidney demonstrates a parapelvic cyst, measuring 3.5 x 1.6 cm, unchanged. The pancreas demonstrates cystic lesions in the neck and junction of the pancreatic body and tail, the largest measuring 1.3 x 1.8 cm, unchanged. There is no adenopathy, free fluid or free air. CT OF THE PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST: A 3.7 x 4.9 cm left adnexal cyst is unchanged. There is sigmoid diverticulosis without diverticulitis. The bladder is largely collapsed with Foley catheter looped inside the bladder. The pelvic bowel loops are unremarkable. There is no inguinal or pelvic adenopathy. There is no pelvic free fluid. OSSEOUS STRUCTURES: Multiple compression fractures of the vertebral column are unchanged. There is multilevel degenerative disease with vacuum disc phenomenon and large anterior osteophytosis and endplate sclerosis. These changes are worst in the lumbar spine. There are no suspicious intraosseous lesions. Multiple acute right-sided rib fractures are present including right fourth through ninth ribs. There are no left-sided rib fractures. IMPRESSION: 1. Multiple acute right rib fractures without evidence of flail chest or segmental fractures. 2. Asymmetric pulmonary edema, right greater than left, with trace right pleural effusion and bibasilar atelectasis. 3.1. 5 cm left lower lobe pulmonary nodule, not included in the field of view of the prior study. If clinically indicated, a three-month followup is recommended. 4. Unchanged left adnexal cyst.

related: predominantly worse on the right side (degree:present)
junction of pancreatic body and tail (location:present)

input: FINDINGS: CT OF THE CHEST WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST: Redemonstrated is a type B aortic dissection with dissection flap originating just distal to the origin of the left subclavian overall unchanged in appearance and extent within the chest since prior examination one day prior. The heart is unremarkable with no evidence of pericardial effusion. The lungs are clear without consolidation or pulmonary nodules. Stable appearance of small bilateral pleural effusions with associated compressive atelectasis. No mediastinal, axillary, or hilar lymphadenopathy. CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST: Redemonstrated is extension of a type B aortic dissection. The celiac axis, SMA, and right renal artery arise from the true lumen. The left renal artery arises from the false lumen. Redemonstrated is an area of soft tissue density immediately adjacent to the aortic bifurcation, adjacent to the dissection flap that upon correlation on multiplanar reformatted images likely represents a component of developing thrombus since _ _ _. However, note should be made that the left common iliac artery, though small area of this dissection flap is seen in this region is mainly supplied by the false lumen with interval appearance of thrombus in this region, is at risk for occlusion. The liver, gallbladder, spleen, pancreas, both adrenal glands, both kidneys, the visualized portions of intra-abdominal small and large bowel are unremarkable. There is no intra-abdominal free air or free fluid. There is no mesenteric or retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy. CT OF THE PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST: The rectum, sigmoid colon, bladder, prostate, and seminal vesicles are unremarkable. There is no pelvic free fluid. There is no pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy. Stable appearance of prominent left external iliac chain lymph node (2:107) measuring 12 x 23 mm. BONE WINDOWS: The visualized osseous structures are unremarkable with no suspicious lytic or sclerotic foci. IMPRESSION: 1. Stable appearance of soft tissue density involving the distal descending aorta at the bifurcation that most likely represents area of thrombus formation since _ _ _. Note that the left common iliac artery is largely supplied by the false lumen and is at risk for occlusion by this area of thrombus formation. Otherwise, unchanged appearance of type B aortic dissection with the left renal artery likely arising from the false lumen. 2. Stable appearance of prominent left external iliac chain lymph node. Findings were discussed with _ _ _ _ _ _ at 11:30 a. m. on _ _ _.

input: FINDINGS: LUNG BASES: The heart is within normal size limits. No pericardial effusion. The distal descending thoracic aorta is normal in course and caliber. The distal esophagus and gastroesophageal junction are grossly unremarkable. No pleural effusions. Evaluation of the lung parenchyma demonstrates minimal dependent, reticular and ground glass opacities compatible with atelectasis. ABDOMEN / PELVIS: The liver is normal in size, contour and attenuation. No focal hepatic mass. No intrahepatic biliary dilatation. The hepatic and portal veins are patent. The gallbladder is unremarkable without evidence of radiopaque gallstones, gallbladder wall thickening or pericholecystic fluid. The adrenal glands are within normal limits. The spleen is normal in size, contour and attenuation. The pancreas is within normal limits without evidence of mass or ductal dilatation. The kidneys are symmetric in size, contour, contrast enhancement and excretion. No focal renal mass, hydronephrosis or nephrolithiasis. Punctate hypo-densities within the renal parenchyma are too small to characterize, however statistically, likely represent renal cysts. The bladder is within normal limits. The uterus is enlarged and boggy in appearance, compatible the patient's postpartum state. No gas is seen within the endometrial cavity. The right and left adnexa are unremarkable. The ovaries are not clearly identified. Small free fluid is present in the right adnexa. The right ovarian vein is patent. The left ovarian vein is not well opacified; however, there is no enlargement or surrounding fat stranding. The stomach is grossly unremarkable. The small bowel is within normal limits without evidence of bowel wall thickening, hyperemia, pneumatosis or obstruction. The colon is normal in course and caliber. There is an excessive amount of colonic stool. The appendix is normal. The abdominal aorta is normal in course and caliber. No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes within the abdomen or pelvis. Note is made of a mildly prominent left paraaortic lymph node measuring 5 mm. Visualized osseous structures are grossly unremarkable. IMPRESSION: 1. ENLARGED POSTPARTUM UTERUS. SMALL FREE FLUID IN THE RIGHT ADNEXA WITH THE OVARIES NOT CLEARLY IDENTIFIED; OTHERWISE, THE ADNEXA ARE UNREMARKABLE. PATENT RIGHT OVARIAN VEIN. THE LEFT OVARIAN VEIN IS NOT WELL OPACIFIED; HOWEVER, THERE IS NO ENLARGEMENT OR SURROUNDING FAT STRANDING TO SUGGEST THROMBOSIS. 2. NORMAL APPENDIX. 3. EXCESSIVE COLONIC STOOL. SUMMARY: 1-NO SIGNIFICANT ABNORMALITY I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: The visualized lung bases demonstrate mosaic attenuation without focal nodules or masses. There are no pleural effusions. The heart is normal in size. The liver is homogeneously low in density, compatible with diffuse fatty infiltration, without focal enhancing masses. In segment 6, there is an irregular hypodense region which may represent invaginated peritoneal fat versus a focus of more increased fatty infiltration. The hepatic vessels are patent. There is no intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation. The patient is status post cholecystectomy. The spleen, pancreas and right adrenal gland are unremarkable. The left adrenal gland is nodular and enlarged; however, it has imaging characteristics compatible with an adenoma. The kidneys are normal in size and enhance symmetrically without hydronephrosis. There is a simple cyst in the inferior pole of the right kidney and a hypodense lesion in the inferior pole of the left kidney which is too small to further characterize. There is no evidence of bowel obstruction or inflammation. In the sigmoid colon, adjacent to an area of surgical clips from history of prior polypectomy, there is narrowing of the lumen which may represent peristalsis. However, an obstructing stricture cannot be excluded, and a colonoscopy is recommended for further evaluation. There is no significant retroperitoneal or pelvic lymphadenopathy. However, there is increased haziness of the mesentery, and prominent mesenteric lymph nodes. This may represent a form of sclerosing mesenteritis / panniculitis. There is no ascites. The uterus is present. A small left ovarian follicle is noted. The urinary bladder is collapsed. The aorta is normal in course and caliber with scattered atherosclerotic calcifications. The inferior vena cava is patent. There is a nonspecific round sclerotic lesion in the L5 vertebral body, likely benign. The remaining visualized osseous structures otherwise demonstrate degenerative changes. IMPRESSION: 1. HAZY MESENTERY WITH PROMINENT MESENTERIC LYMPH NODES, POSSIBLY REPRESENTING SCLEROSING MESENTERITIS. 2. FOCAL AREA OF NARROWING IN THE SIGMOID COLON WHICH MAY REPRESENT PERISTALSIS. HOWEVER, A MASS OR STRICTURE IS NOT EXCLUDED AND A COLONOSCOPY IS RECOMMENDED FOR FURTHER EVALUATION. 3. HEPATIC STEATOSIS. 4. LEFT ADRENAL ADENOMA. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: The visualized lung bases are unremarkable. There is hypertrophy of the caudate lobe suggestive of possible underlying cirrhosis. However, there is no definite surface nodularity. There are a few tiny hypervascular foci seen only on the arterial phase without evidence of washout. These likely represent small arterial portal shunts. There are no focal hepatic lesions of concern. The spleen is markedly enlarged, measuring 18 cm in craniocaudal dimension. There are peripheral areas of low attenuation in the spleen on the arterial phase which fill in on the more delayed phases of imaging. This is likely related to differential enhancement of the spleen. There is a suggestion of small esophageal varices on series 6, image 43. There are markedly enlarged right lower quadrant mesenteric varices compatible with portal hypertension. Some of these varices actually decompress into the IVC. The splenic vein, splenic portal confluence, and portal venous system are all patent. The gallbladder, pancreas, adrenal glands are unremarkable. The left kidney is displaced by the enlarged spleen. Otherwise, the kidneys are unremarkable. The bladder is unremarkable. The prostate gland is asymetrically enlarged with more prominence of the right side. The stomach is grossy unremarkable. There is colonic diverticulosis without evidence of diverticulitis. The appendix is normal. There are no pathologically enlarged lymph nodes in the abdomen. Multilevel degenerative changes are noted in the visualized lumbar spine. There are no suspicious focal osseous lesions. There is no abdominal ascites. IMPRESSION: 1. OVERALL FINDINGS ARE COMPATIBLE WITH PORTAL HYPERTENSION WITH MARKED SPLENOMEGALY, SMALL ESOPHAGEAL VARICES, AND LARGE RIGHT LOWER QUADRANT MESENTERIC VARICES. THERE IS RELATIVE CAUDATE LOBE HYPERTROPHY WHICH IS SUGGESTIVE OF UNDERLYING CIRRHOSIS; HOWEVER, THERE IS NO DEFINITE NODULARITY OF THE LIVER SURFACE. 2. NO FOCAL HEPATIC LESION OF CONCERN. A FEW SMALL HYPERVASCULAR FOCI IN THE LIVER THAT ARE ONLY SEEN ON ARTERIAL PHASE, MOST LIKELY REPRESENTING ARTERIOPORTAL SHUNTS. ATTENTION ON FOLLOW-UP. 3. DIVERTICULOSIS. 4. ASYMMETRIC RIGHT SIDED PROSTATIC ENLARGEMENT. RECOMMEND CLINICAL CORRELATION. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDINGS, MAY NED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: The visualized lung bases are well aerated. The liver is normal in attenuation without discrete lesion or biliary dilatation. There is an angiographic area of diminished attenuation in the anterolateral aspect of the medial left lobe, compatible with steatosis. The spleen, pancreas, and adrenal glands are unremarkable. The gallbladder is present and appears unremarkable. The kidneys symmetrically and homogeneously enhance in this precontrast without discrete lesions or hydronephrosis. The urinary bladder appears unremarkable. Small and large bowel are of normal caliber without evidence of obstruction or inflammation. The uterus is markedly enlarged with multiple masses compatible with leiomyomata. Within the anterior uterine myometrium, there are multiple round areas of hypoattenuation. For example, there is a 4.3 x 2.2-cm oblong, ovoid area of hypoattenuation in the anterior myometrium (series 4, image # 246). This could be postoperative in nature. Additionally, within a hypoattenuating region, there is a round hyperdense focus on series 4, image # 239, which has similar attenuation to the vascular structures. Additionally, anterior and inferior to the uterus and superior to the urinary bladder, there is a rim-enhancing collection with fluid collection with foci of gas, concerning for an abscess. The collection measures 5.9 x 3.9-cm in axial dimension and 5.8-cm in craniocaudal dimension (series 3, image # 70). There is also fluid within the cul-de-sac, which is greater than water attenuation and may be due to the presence of blood or pus. There is no regional adenopathy. Regional osseous structures appear unremarkable. IMPRESSION: 1. RIM-ENHANCING PELVIC FLUID COLLECTION WITH PRESENCE OF GAS, CONCERNING FOR ABSCESS. 2. HYPOATTENUATING REGIONS IN THE ANTERIOR MYOMETRIUM MAY BE POSTOPERATIVE IN NATURE, OR DUE TO DEGENERATING / NECROTIC FIBROIDS. THERE IS A FOCUS OF HYPERATTENUATION WITHIN ONE OF THE LOW DENSITY AREAS, WITH ATTENUATION SIMILAR TO VASCULATURE. THIS MAY REPRESENT AN AREA OF VASCULAR INJURY / PSEUDOANEURYSM, WHICH COULD BE FURTHER EVALUATED WITH ULTRASOUND IF CLINICALLY INDICATED. PRELIMINARY FINDINGS WERE DISCUSSED WITH MD Spencer Adaline ON OCTOBER 29, 2009 AT 0500 HOURS, WHICH DID NOT DIFFER FROM THE FINAL INTERPRETATION. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDINGS, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: Lung bases: Small bilateral pleural effusions. Bilateral patchy ground glass opacities. Granuloma at the right base. The liver is shrunken with a markedly nodular contour and widening of the fissures consistent with cirrhosis. There is hypertrophy of the left hepatic lobe relative to the right. No hypervascular lesions with washout appreciated to suggest HCC. No intra-or extrahepatic biliary dilatation. The portal and hepatic veins are patent. There is recanalization of the paraumbilical vein. There are markedly enlarged portosystemic collaterals arising from the SMV and extending into the right renal vein. Small esophageal varices are present. Diffuse fat stranding consistent with anasarca. The gallbladder is decompressed with multiple gallstones. The pancreas is unremarkable. The spleen is enlarged measuring 14.6 cm in anterior-posterior dimension. The bilateral adrenal glands are unremarkable. The left kidney is unremarkable. Within the superior posterior pole of the right kidney there is decreased attenuation on all phases of contrast (2/53 and 4/40) with a fairly sharp demarkation to normal kidney. The stomach and small bowel are unremarkable. Scattered diverticula in the sigmoid colon without evidence of bowel wall thickening or inflammatory stranding. There is a moderate amount of fluid within the colon. Within the proximal ascending colon there is a linear foreign body (2/185) measuring approximately 2.8 cm in length. The foreign body appears to be completely within the colon demonstrates a HU of approximately 280. The appendix is normal. The bladder is mildly distended with an air-fluid level, likely iatrogenic. The prostate gland is normal. The aorta is normal in caliber. There is a separate origin of the common hepatic artery from the aorta. The IVC is patent. No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes within the abdomen or pelvis. Small umbilical hernia containing fluid. There is mild superior endplate compression deformity of the L2 vertebral body of approximately 15%, with approximately 6 mm of retropulsion of the superior posterior fracture fragment into the spinal canal. Mild degenerative endplate changes at L5-S1. IMPRESSION: 1. CIRRHOSIS WITH THE STIGMATA OF PORTAL HYPERTENSION INCLUDING ASCITES, SPLENOMEGALY, LARGE PORTOSYSTEMIC COLLATERALS, AND ESOPHAGEAL VARICES. NO SUSPICIOUS HYPERVASCULAR LESIONS WITH WASHOUT TO SUGGEST HCC. THE COMMON HEPATIC ARTERY ARISES DIRECTLY FROM THE AORTA, AND THE PORTAL AND HEPATIC VEINS ARE PATENT. 2. LINEAR FOREIGN BODY WITHIN THE ASCENDING COLON. THE DENSITY IS NOT HIGH ENOUGH TO BE METAL, AND MAY REPRESENT A TOOTHPICK. 3. DECREASED ATTENUATION WITHIN THE SUPERIOR POSTERIOR POLE OF THE RIGHT KIDNEY WITH A FAIRLY SHARP DEMARCATION WITH THE NORMAL KIDNEY. THIS LIKELY REPRESENTS A SEQUELA OF INFARCT, HOWEVER A NONENHANCING MASS IS NOT ENTIRELY EXCLUDED. CONSIDER FOLLOW-UP IMAGING IN 3 MONTHS TO ASSESS FOR STABILITY OF FINDINGS. 4. DIVERTICULOSIS WITHOUT EVIDENCE OF DIVERTICULITIS. 5. MILD SUPERIOR ENDPLATE COMPRESSION OF FORMERLY OF THE L2 VERTEBRAL BODY WITH MILD RETROPULSION (6 MM) INTO THE SPINAL CANAL. 6. CHOLELITHIASIS. 7. GAS WITHIN THE BLADDER LUMEN, LIKELY IATROGENIC, HOWEVER CORRELATE CLINICALLY FOR THE POSSIBILITY OF INFECTION. 8. FINDINGS DISCUSSED WITH Bell Jaxton AT 11 AM ON 3-7-2001. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLY SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: There is no evidence of mesenteric ischemia. The mesenteric vessels are patent. There is no bowel wall thickening. Incidentally noted is a small 1.7 cm enhancing nodule adjacent to a small bowel loop in the anterior abdomen, series 5, image 45. This is of uncertain etiology. The visualized lung bases show no mass or effusion. The liver contains a small cyst in the posterior right lobe. Gallbladder is grossly unremarkable. Spleen is normal in size. There is a small amount of perisplenic and perihepatic ascites. Pancreas demonstrates relative fatty atrophy. Both adrenal glands are unremarkable. Left adrenal gland appears thickened, possibly due to hyperplasia. Both kidneys are unremarkable. There is a small amount of fluid along the left pericolic gutter. The prostate gland is enlarged. Degenerative spine changes are noted. There are bilateral pars defects with mild anterolisthesis of L5 on S1. IMPRESSION: 1. NO EVIDENCE OF MESENTERIC ISCHEMIA. 2. SLIGHT DILATATION OF JEJUNAL SMALL BOWEL LOOPS AND COLLAPSED ILEAL LOOPS. A SMALL BOWEL OBSTRUCTION CANNOT BE EXCLUDED. RECOMMEND SMALL BOWEL FOLLOW-THROUGH TO FURTHER ASSESS. 3. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDINGS, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: Examination is limited by the lack of intravenous contrast. The heart is within normal size limits. No pericardial effusion. The distal descending thoracic aorta is normal in course and caliber. The distal esophagus and gastroesophageal junction are grossly unremarkable. No pleural effusions. Evaluation of the lung parenchyma demonstrates minimal dependent, reticular and ground glass opacities compatible with atelectasis. The liver is normal in size, contour and attenuation. No large hepatic mass. No prominent intrahepatic biliary dilatation. The gallbladder is unremarkable without evidence of radiopaque gallstones, gallbladder wall thickening or pericholecystic fluid. The adrenal glands are within normal limits. The spleen is normal in size, contour and attenuation. The pancreas is grossly within normal limits. The kidneys are symmetric in size. No gross renal mass, hydronephrosis or nephrolithiasis. The bladder, prostate and seminal vesicles are within normal limits. The stomach is grossly unremarkable. The small bowel is within normal limits without evidence of bowel wall thickening, pneumatosis or obstruction. The colon is normal in course and caliber. Scattered diverticula are present without evidence of diverticulitis. There is mild mucosal hypertrophy of the sigmoid colon in the setting of diverticular disease. The appendix is within normal limits. No free fluid within the abdomen or pelvis. The abdominal aorta is normal in course and caliber. There is focal dilatation of the right external iliac vein measuring up to 2.0 cm with an incompletely evaluated, more proximal area of short segment luminal narrowing series 3, image 260. No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes within the abdomen or pelvis. Evaluation of the visualized osseous structures demonstrate minimal degenerative changes involving the spine. IMPRESSION: 1. NO IMAGING ETIOLOGY FOR THE PATIENT'S RIGHT FLANK PAIN. SPECIFICALLY, NO UROLITHIASIS OR APPENDICITIS. 2. FOCAL DILATATION OF THE RIGHT EXTERNAL ILIAC VEIN WITH AN ASSOCIATED MORE PROXIMAL, SHORT SEGMENT RIGHT EXTERNAL ILIAC VENOUS NARROWING WHICH MAY BE DUE TO REMOTE THROMBUS OR STRICTURE. THROMBUS WITHIN THE DILATED EXTERNAL ILIAC VEIN IS NOT EXCLUDED. RECOMMEND FURTHER EVALUATION WITH A CT VENOGRAM. 3. DIVERTICULOSIS WITHOUT EVIDENCE OF DIVERTICULITIS. 4. PRELIMINARY REPORT PROVIDED BY MD Lyric L., RADIOLOGY RESIDENT ON CALL. FINAL RESULTS AND RECOMMENDATIONS DISCUSSED WITH MD DAISY VANNIEUWENHOVEN, THE ED CALLBACK NURSE ON 12-29-18 APPROXIMATELY 9:40 A. M. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLY SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: Diffusely distended loops of small and large bowel, without transition point identified, and with proportionate distention of the multiple loops visualized, most compatible with postoperative ileus; no evidence of intestinal obstruction. Evaluation of the visceral organs, vasculature, and soft tissues is limited secondary to lack of intravenous contrast, and evaluation of the pelvis is limited secondary to streak / beam-hardening artifact from right total hip arthroplasty. Accounting for this, no abnormal bowel wall thickening, and no free intraperitoneal air to suggest bowel perforation. Liver and spleen are normal in size and contour. Gallbladder is without pericholecystic fluid or wall thickening. No biliary ductal dilatation. Fatty involution of the pancreas. Normal adrenals. Bilateral renal cortical thinning with nonspecific bilateral perinephric stranding; no hydroureteronephrosis or nephroureterolithiasis. Urinary bladder collapsed; mild prostatomegaly. No lymphadenopathy or ascites. Abdominal aorta nonaneurysmal; mild vascular calcifications. Small focus of right-sided retroperitoneal gas, most compatible with recent postsurgical changes. Mild bibasilar atelectasis. No pleural or pericardial effusions. Heart size within normal limits. Coronary artery calcifications. Status post prior cardiac surgery / median sternotomy. Status post posterior spinal fusion at L2--L5, with bilateral transpedicular screws at L2, L4, and L5; intervertebral body spacers at L2--L3 and L4--L5; and L3--L4 fusion. Bony alignment near-anatomic. Bone graft harvest site at the right lateral iliac wing, with post-surgical material noted more laterally within the adjacent soft tissues. IMPRESSION: 1. Dilated small bowel likely related to postoperative ileus. 2. Postsurgical changes status post recent lumbar spine surgery. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: Small bilateral pleural effusions are seen with adjacent areas of compressive atelectasis. Lung bases are otherwise clear. A small amount of ascites is seen within the abdomen. A small amount of free air is also seen anteriorly, refer to series 2, image 19, which may be postoperative in nature. A nasogastric tube is seen with tip in the stomach and side port near the gastroesophageal junction. The spleen, pancreas, gallbladder, kidneys, adrenal glands and stomach are unremarkable. Multiple dilated loops of small bowel are seen within the abdomen, with a maximum diameter of 4.9 cm. The dilated loops of bowel terminate relatively abruptly in the region of the ileal ileo-anastomosis in the right lower quadrant, refer to series 2, image 62, which directly underlies the site of the previous ileostomy. Mild inflammatory changes are seen in the overlying soft tissues, consistent with history of recent ileostomy takedown. The small bowel distal to the site of the anastomosis appears decompressed. Contrast material is seen throughout the colon, consistent with history of recent barium enema. The colon itself appears relatively unremarkable. Soft tissue density is again seen in the presacral area, stable from prior exam, and likely reflecting post-treatment change. Suture material is seen within the rectum, consistent with history of prior rectal surgery. Multiple nonpathologically enlarged lymph nodes are seen within the paracaval and portacaval regions. A small amount of free fluid is seen within the pelvis. Two small sclerotic foci are noted within the left ilium, likely reflecting bone islands. IMPRESSION: 1. MULTIPLE DILATED LOOPS OF SMALL BOWEL WITH ABRUPT TRANSITION POINT IN THE REGION OF THE ILEAL ILEO-ANASTOMOSIS. GAS IS SEEN DISTALLY, SUGGESTIVE OF PARTIAL, MODERATE GRADE BOWEL OBSTRUCTION. 2. SMALL AMOUNT OF INTRA-ABDOMINAL FREE AIR, WHICH MAY BE POSTOPERATIVE IN NATURE. RECOMMEND ATTENTION ON FOLLOW-UP IMAGING. 3. SMALL BILATERAL PLEURAL EFFUSIONS WITH ADJACENT COMPRESSIVE ATELECTASIS. 4. STABLE PRESACRAL SOFT TISSUE DENSITY, PRESUMABLY REFLECTING POST-TREATMENT CHANGE. 5. NASOGASTRIC TUBE WITH TIP IN THE STOMACH AND SIDE PORT NEAR THE GE JUNCTION, WHICH CAN BE SAFELY ADVANCED 5-10 CM. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDINGS, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: The visualized portions of the lower thorax demonstrate multiple pulmonary nodules, some of which appear calcified. The largest is noted in the left lower lobe (4, 19) measuring 3 mm in size. An additional more irregular shaped nodular opacity measures 5 mm in size. There are on pleural or pericardial effusions. The liver is normal in size and contour. Within the left hepatic lobe spanning segments 2/3 and 4a, there is a hypodense lesion, which measures 3.6 x 3.4 cm in size and narrows the left hepatic vein and displaces and slightly narrows the left portal vein. It results in biliary dilatation distal to the lesion. Additionally, there is a THAD surrounding the lesion due to alteration in the hepatic blood flow. The left portal vein has an apparent filling defect within it just adjacent to this mass (7, 44) that is likely secondary to branching of the vein rather than portal vein thrombus. This is also well imaged on Image 96-97 on Series 6. Just superolateral to this lesion, there is an additional hypodense focus measuring 9 mm in size that could represent a small satellite lesion. A low attenuation lymph node is noted in the porta hepatis measuring 1.3 cm in short axis. Additional subcentimeter retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy is also noted. The gallbladder appears unremarkable. The pancreas, spleen, and adrenal glands are within normal limits. There is a 2.4 cm thin walled fluid structure in the mid left kidney compatible with a simple cyst. The kidneys otherwise, appear unremarkable. The bowel is normal in caliber. There is no evidence of obstruction. There is no free fluid. The bladder is unremarkable. A lucency is noted in the right femur measuring 1.2 cm in size compatible with a synovial herniation pit. No other lytic or blastic lesions are identified in the visualized osseous structures. There are degenerative changes of the spine. IMPRESSION: 1.3. 6 X 3.4 CM LOW ATTENUATION LEFT HEPATIC LOBE LESION, AS DESCRIBED ABOVE WITH POSSIBLE SATELLITE LESION MEASURING 9 MM, ALSO WITHIN THE LEFT HEPATIC LOBE. NO LESIONS IDENTIFIED IN THE RIGHT HEPATIC LOBE. THIS COULD BE COMPATIBLE WITH REPORTED HISTORY OF CHOLANGIOCARCINOMA, BUT DIFFERENTIAL DIAGNOSIS WOULD ALSO INCLUDE METASTASIS. 2. PARTIALLY NECROTICPORTA HEPATIS LYMPH NODE MEASURING 1.3 CM IN SHORT AXIS. 3. MULTIPLE PULMONARY NODULES MEASURING UP TO 3-5 MM IN SIZE, AS DESCRIBED ABOVE. RECOMMEND COMPARISON TO PRIOR OUTSIDE IMAGING AND ATTENTION TO THESE ON FOLLOW-UP EXAMINATIONS. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLY SIGNIFICANT FINDING; MAY NEED ACTION. I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: The lung bases are without pleural effusions. There is minimal dependent atelectasis. No focal concerning pulmonary nodules. The heart is not enlarged. There is no pericardial effusion. The liver enhances homogeneously. There is no focal intrahepatic mass. The portal veins and hepatic veins are patent. No nodularity to the hepatic capsule. The Gallbladder Is Fluid-Filled and Unremarkable. No Radiopaque Gallstones Are Present. The Adrenals Are without Focal Mass or Nodularity. The Bilateral Kidneys Demonstrate Symmetric Homogeneous Enhancement. No Solid Appearing Renal Masses. No Renal Cyst. No stones. No hydronephrosis or hydroureter involving either kidney. The spleen is not enlarged. There is no focal splenic mass. The splenic vein is patent. The pancreas demonstrates homogeneous enhancement. There is no ductal dilatation. No peripancreatic stranding. The visualized GE junction is without evidence of a sliding type hiatal hernia. The small bowel are unremarkable without evidence of dilatation or focal inflammatory change to suggest underlying obstruction or gastric colitis. An air-filled normal caliber appendix is present in the right lower quadrant. There are prominent right lower quadrant lymph nodes seen measuring up to 7 x 15 mm, 8 x 13 mm seen in the vicinity of the appendix and anterior to the psoas muscle. Additionally, there may be some subtle mesenteric inflammation in this area. Findings may indicate underlying mesenteric adenitis in the appropriate clinical setting. No free fluid is seen in the pelvis. The visualized colonic bowel loops are stool filled throughout without evidence of significant inflammatory change. The bladder demonstrates no significant wall thickening. No additional areas of concerning adenopathy is identified aside from the right lower quadrant likely reactive nodes. There are some subcentimeter scattered mesenteric and para-aortic nodes present. The visualized bones are without areas of concerning sclerotic or lytic bony lesions. The soft tissues are without focal mass. IMPRESSION: 1. A NORMAL AIR FILLED APPENDIX IS PRESENT IN THE RIGHT LOWER QUADRANT. 2. PROMINENT RIGHT LOWER QUADRANT LYMPH NODES ARE SEEN WITH SOME MILD MESENTERIC INFLAMMATORY CHANGE. FINDINGS MAY INDICATE MESENTERIC ADENITIS IN THE PROPER CLINICAL SETTING. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

The lung bases are clear, without evidence of focal consolidation or a discrete nodule. There is no pleural effusion or pneumothorax. ABDOMEN / PELVIS: The liver is normal in appearance, without evidence of a focal liver lesion. There is no intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary dilatation, with a normal appearing gallbladder. The spleen measures 11 cm in AP diameter, and is normal in appearance, with an immediately adjacent 6 mm splenule (3/159). The pancreas is normal in appearance, without evidence of a pancreatic mass or ductal dilatation. The right adrenal gland is normal, without a nodule or mass. There is a 8 x 10 mm left adrenal nodule, with a postcontrast Hounsfield attenuation of 38, and Hounsfield attenuation of 29 on the delayed images. This nodule is stable compared to 2007. The stomach and duodenum are in normal anatomic positions. Small bowel loops are normal in caliber, without evidence of dilatation or wall thickening. The colon is normal in appearance, without evidence of diverticulosis, diverticulitis, pneumatosis, pericolonic fat stranding, or diverticulitis. The terminal ileum is normal in appearance, while a normal appendix is identified in the deep pelvis (3/344). The bladder is normal in appearance, without evidence of wall thickening, mass, or a stone. The prostate and seminal vesicles are normal. Similar to prior examination, there are no enlarged inguinal, iliac chain, or pelvic lymph nodes by CT size criteria. There are no enlarged retroperitoneal lymph nodes or mesenteric lymph nodes in the abdomen. Several small left periaortic lymph nodes are stable in size (3/111, 3/156). No grossly enlarged lymph nodes are identified. The portal veins are patent and normal in size. The splenic vein and SMV are patent. input: FINDINGS: The heart is within normal size limits. Tiny pericardial effusion. The distal descending thoracic aorta is normal in course and caliber. The distal esophagus and gastroesophageal junction are grossly unremarkable. No pleural effusions. Evaluation of the lung bases demonstrates several tiny pulmonary lung nodules, better described on the CT thorax report from the prior day. Tiny calcified granuloma in the left lung base. The liver is normal in size, contour and attenuation. No focal hepatic mass. No intrahepatic biliary dilatation. The hepatic and portal veins are patent. The gallbladder is unremarkable without evidence of radiopaque gallstones, gallbladder wall thickening or pericholecystic fluid. The common bile duct is normal. The adrenal glands are within normal limits with no focal masses. The spleen is normal in size, contour and attenuation. The pancreas is within normal limits without evidence of mass or ductal dilatation. The kidneys are symmetric in size, contour, contrast enhancement and excretion. No focal renal mass, hydronephrosis or nephrolithiasis. The bladder is unremarkable without evidence of tumor recurrence. Heterogeneous appearance of the uterus compatible with known endometrial cancer. Again seen are several uterine fibroids with focal areas of calcification. The stomach is grossly unremarkable. The small bowel is within normal limits without evidence of bowel wall thickening, hyperemia, pneumatosis or obstruction. The colon is normal in course and caliber. The appendix is within normal limits. No free fluid within the abdomen or pelvis. The peritoneum is unremarkable. The abdominal aorta is normal in course and caliber. Small slightly prominent retroperitoneal lymph nodes are again seen, and unchanged since 7/23/2007. Visualized osseous demonstrate mild degenerative changes without evidence of osseous metastases. IMPRESSION: 1. HETEROGENEOUS APPEARANCE OF THE UTERUS COMPATIBLE WITH KNOWN ENDOMETRIAL CANCER. 2. STABLE APPEARING UTERINE FIBROIDS WITH FOCAL AREAS OF CALCIFICATION. 3. SEVERAL SMALL LUNG NODULES IN THE LUNG BASES COMPATIBLE WITH METASTATIC DISEASE, BETTER CHARACTERIZED ON PRIOR CT OF THE THORAX. 4. NO EVIDENCE OF LIVER, ADRENAL, RENAL, OR OSSEOUS METASTASES. 5. MULTIPLE STABLE SLIGHTLY PROMINENT RETROPERITONEAL LYMPH NODES. SUMMARY: 2-ABNORMAL, PREVIOUSLY REPORTED I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: Areas of subpleural reticulation within the visualized lung bases are redemonstrated. Tiny pleural effusions are present bilaterally with compression atelectasis. Multiple calcified pleural plaques are redemonstrated. Areas of scarring within the visualized lung bases are also redemonstrated. The visualized heart and thoracic vasculature are unremarkable. There is periportal edema present. Pneumobilia within the left liver lobe is redemonstrated, presumably related to prior sphincterotomy. A heterogeneous region within the peripheral aspect of the left liver lobe is present, presumably postsurgical. There has been interval development of extensive portal venous thrombosis. This includes a large amount of nonocclusive thrombus within the distal main portal vein, which extends to the portal splenic confluence. There is complete occlusion of the left portal vein and anterior division of the right portal vein. There are also multiple areas of nonocclusive thrombus within the right posterior portal vein branches. A trace amount of nonocclusive thrombus within the superior mesenteric artery is also noted. The superior mesenteric and splenic veins are patent. Atherosclerotic disease of the abdominal aorta and iliac vessels is redemonstrated. The spleen and adrenal glands are unremarkable. Again noted are bilateral renal cysts, some of which are large with peripheral calcifications. These are unchanged. Bilateral extrarenal pelves are present. Mild cortical thinning is unchanged. Postsurgical changes are redemonstrated, status-post Whipple, including hepatojejunostomy and gastrojejunostomy. No drainable fluid collection is identified. A tiny, residual collection of fluid near the surgical sites is now barely perceptible. An area of loculated fat within the right upper quadrant is redemonstrated. There has been interval increase in free fluid, which is still mild and visualized in the perihepatic, perisplenic, bilateral paracolic gutters, and pelvic regions. No loculated collections are identified. Numerous colonic diverticula are redemonstrated, without diverticulitis. The visualized bowel is otherwise unremarkable without obstruction. No definite colonic wall thickening or inflammatory changes are noted, though evaluation of bowel mucosa is somewhat limited due to enteric contrast. The appendix is contrast-filled and unremarkable. The urinary bladder is unremarkable. An enlarged prostate gland is again noted. Degenerative changes throughout the visualized osseous structures are redemonstrated. No new suspicious focal bony lesion is identified. IMPRESSION: 1. interval development of extensive portal venous thrombus, including NONOCCLUSIVE thrombus in the distal main portal vein extending to the portal splenic confluence, complete occlusion of the left main portal vein AND ANTERIOR BRANCH OF THE RIGHT PORTAL VEIN, and multiple areas of nonocclusive thrombus within the right portal venous branches. Tiny, nonocclusive superior mesenteric artery thrombosiS. This was discussed with Dr. Flynn Marcos on 10-7-2004 at approximately 1800 hours. 2. Stable postsurgical changes, with no drainable fluid collection identified. 3. No CT evidence of colitis, although evaluation of the mucosa is limited by enteric contrast material. 4. Interval increase in small, non loculated ascites. 5. Multiple Non-emergent FINDINGS as described. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT ABNORMALITY / CHANGE, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

related: anterior division of right portal vein (location:present)

input: FINDINGS: The visualized lung bases show a calcified granuloma in the right lower lobe. No pleural effusions seen. The visualized portion of the heart is within normal limits. No evidence of esophageal varices is noted. The liver contour is nodular, consistent with underlying cirrhosis. A new area of hypervascularity is seen involving segment VII-VIII on series 4 image 34 without associated washout. No additional lesions are seen. No delayed washout lesions are present. A TIPS is present extending from the right hepatic vein into the main portal vein. It remains patent. Multiple gallstones are again seen, though without pericholecystic stranding. The pancreas and the adrenal glands are unremarkable in appearance. The spleen is enlarged measuring 14 cm in craniocaudal dimension. The left kidney is unremarkable. The right kidney at the superior pole again has a large exophytic cyst measuring 5.2 cm in diameter. A second cyst is noted at the interpolar region, though which is slightly increased in size measuring approximately 7 mm in diameter. Atherosclerotic disease of the abdominal aorta is noted. Conventional hepatic arterial supply is present. Single renal artery supplies the kidneys. IVC is patent. Recanalized paraumbilical vein is noted. Extensive splenorenal shunts are present. Gastric varices are also noted. The small bowel and the colon are otherwise unremarkable. The urinary bladder and the prostate gland are within normal limits. Lymph nodes of the hepatoduodenal ligament as well as the portocaval space remain stable in size and number. The osseous structures are otherwise unremarkable. Again seen is the curvilinear high-density material abutting the IVC, which may represent previous site of mesocaval shunt. IMPRESSION: 1. INTERVAL DEVELOPMENT OF A HYPERVASCULAR FOCUS THAT IS SOMEWHAT LINEAR IN SHAPE INVOLVING SEGMENT VII-VIII JUNCTION WITHOUT ASSOCIATED WASHOUT. ALTHOUGH THIS MAY REPRESENT ARTERIOPORTAL SHUNT, ATTENTION ON FOLLOW-UP STUDY IS RECOMMENDED TO ENSURE STABILITY. 2. CIRRHOSIS WITH PORTAL HYPERTENSION WITH A PATENT TIPS. 3. TWO RIGHT RENAL CYSTS. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDINGS, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: The heart is normal in size. No pericardial effusion. The distal descending thoracic aorta is normal in course and caliber. Small hiatal hernia noted. No pleural effusions. Subsegmental atelectasis at both lung bases. Mild diffuse hepatic steatosis noted. No focal hepatic mass. No intrahepatic biliary dilatation. The hepatic and portal veins are patent. The gallbladder is normal without evidence of radiopaque gallstones, gallbladder wall thickening or pericholecystic fluid. The common bile duct is normal. The adrenal glands appear normal. The spleen is normal in size and attenuation. The pancreas is normal without evidence of mass or ductal dilatation. Punctate nonobstructing calculus in the interpolar region of the right kidney (series 3, image 148). Additional adjacent 4-mm nonobstructing calculus in the right kidney (series 3, image 152). Area of focal scarring in this region also noted in the superior pole of the right kidney (series 3, image 149). No hydronephrosis bilaterally. Subcentimeter hypoattenuating bilateral renal lesions are well-defined, however, are too small to fully characterize. Bladder is normal. Uterus is absent. Small amount of dense fluid in the pelvis, consistent with hemoperitoneum. Small amount of fluid around the right adnexa. Possible right ovarian cyst (series 3, image 317). The stomach is grossly normal. The small bowel is normal without evidence of bowel wall thickening, hyperemia, pneumatosis or obstruction. The colon is normal in course and caliber. The appendix is within normal limits. Abdominal aorta is non-aneurysmal. No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes within the abdomen or pelvis. Visualized osseous structures are grossly normal. IMPRESSION: 1. SMALL VOLUME OF HEMOPERITONEUM IN THE PELVIS MAY BE RELATED TO A RUPTURED RIGHT OVARIAN HEMORRHAGIC CYST. ULTRASOUND CORRELATION MAY BE OBTAINED IF CLINICALLY INDICATED. 2. RIGHT RENAL NON-OBSTRUCTING CALCULI MEASURING UP TO 4 MM. NO HYDRONEPHROSIS AND NO OBSTRUCTING URETERAL CALCULI SEEN. 3. NORMAL APPENDIX. 4. MILD DIFFUSE HEPATIC STEATOSIS. CORRELATE WITH LIVER FUNCTION TESTS. These significant findings were conveyed to the ED callback nurse on Stafford Paris on 12-30-01 at 930pm. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLY SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: The visualized lung bases demonstrate stable moderate bilateral pleural effusions with associated consolidations of the lower lobes. The heart is enlarged. Since the prior study, there has been interval placement of AICD / defibrillator leads with tips terminating in the right atrium and right ventricle. A small pericardial effusion is redemonstrated. Bilateral gynecomastia is present. The liver is markedly heterogeneous in enhancement, with peripheral wedge-shaped areas of lower attenuation predominantly in the right hepatic lobe, likely representing delayed perfusion. The hepatic veins are not well opacified. The portal veins are patent, but the right portal vein is very diminutive. Conventional hepatic arterial anatomy is identified. There is no intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation. The gallbladder is surgically absent. There is redemonstration of several loculated fluid collections surrounding the pancreatic body and tail compatible with pancreatic pseudocysts, again measuring approximately 7.2 x 11.3 cm, with extensive surrounding peripancreatic inflammatory changes. Multiple smaller pseudocysts are seen in the lesser sac and around the gastrohepatic ligament. Residual enhancing pancreatic parenchyma is seen at the pancreatic head and uncinate process. The splenic vein is not visualized compatible with thrombosis. Since the prior study, there has been interval enlargement of the spleen with multiple wedge shaped peripheral hypoattenuating areas, compatible with splenic infarction. The margin of the spleen is ill-defined, likely representing a subcapsular hematoma. The superior mesenteric vein is patent. The adrenal glands are unremarkable. The kidneys are normal in size without hydronephrosis. There is no evidence of bowel obstruction or inflammation. A nasogastric tube is seen with its tip in the fundus of the stomach. There are no pathologically enlarged retroperitoneal, mesenteric or pelvic lymph nodes. There is a large amount of ascites as before. The prostate is normal in size. The urinary bladder is collapsed around a Foley catheter. The aorta is normal in course and caliber. The inferior vena cava is patent. A right common femoral central venous line is present. The visualized osseous structures are unremarkable. Severe anasarca is present. IMPRESSION: 1. NEW AREAS OF INFARCTION OF THE SPLEEN AND A SUBCAPSULAR HEMATOMA. 2. MARKEDLY HETEROGENEOUS ENHANCEMENT OF THE RIGHT HEPATIC LOBE LIKELY DUE TO DELAYED PERFUSION. DIMINUTIVE BUT PATENT RIGHT PORTAL VEIN. HEPATIC VEINS NOT WELL OPACIFIED. 3. REDEMONSTRATION OF PANCREATIC PSEUDOCYSTS AND EXTENSIVE SURROUNDING PERIPANCREATIC INFLAMMATORY CHANGES COMPATIBLE WITH THE PATIENT'S KNOWN HISTORY OF PANCREATITIS. CHRONIC SPLENIC VEIN THROMBOSIS LIKELY RELATED TO PANCREATITIS. 4. MODERATE BILATERAL PLEURAL EFFUSIONS, MARKED ASCITES AND ANASARCA AS BEFORE. 5. BILATERAL LOWER LOBE CONSOLIDATIONS, POSSIBLY REPRESENTING ASPIRATION / INFECTION. 6. INTERVAL PLACEMENT A DUAL LEAD AICD / PACEMAKER, NASOGASTRIC TUBE AND RIGHT FEMORAL CENTRAL VENOUS LINE. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: Decreased right pleural effusion, now trace, with minimal associated compressive atelectasis. The visualized airways are patent without evidence of dilation or bronchial wall thickening. The heart appears normal in size. No pericardial effusion is demonstrated. Status post nonanatomic, subsegmental wedge resection of metastatic lesions in the liver as well as resection of segments 5 and 6. Multiple round post-surgical hypodense lesions with internal foci of gas are present, unchanged in size and appearance from the prior study, compatible with Surgicel packing. The 4-cm lesion in segment 4 still has a small air-fluid level, likely related to Surgicel packing. Small right posterior subphrenic fluid collection, slightly increased in size from 13 mm to 20 mm in greatest diameter. This fluid collection is causing new mass-effect on the adjacent hepatic parenchyma. Tiny foci of gas are seen within this fluid collection which may be from the adjacent Surgicel material versus new developing abscess. Omentum appears to have been manipulated to be adherent to the surgical resection site of segments 5 and 6. Trace perihepatic ascites remains. No free intraperitoneal air. Right anterior approach abdominal drain with tip inferior to the liver. The pancreas appears normal. No pancreatic ductal dilation is present. The spleen is normal in size and homogeneous in attenuation. The splenic vein is patent. The adrenal glands appear normal. Stable partially exophytic left renal cyst.. The urinary bladder demonstrates normal wall thickness. The uterus and adnexa appear normal. The abdominal aorta is patent and normal in caliber. The celiac, superior mesenteric, renal, inferior mesenteric, and iliac arteries are patent.. There are no enlarged lymph nodes in the abdomen, pelvis, or inguinal regions by CT size criteria.. Status post lower anterior resection with reanastomosis. The bowel is normal in caliber, wall thickness and mucosal enhancement pattern. Postsurgical changes in the anterior abdominal wall. Osseous structures are within normal limits. IMPRESSION: 1. STATUS POST NONANATOMIC, SUBSEGMENTAL WEDGE RESECTION OF METASTATIC HEPATIC LESIONS WITH SURGICEL PACKING. THE RESECTION CAVITIES HAVE NOT CHANGED IN SIZE OR APPEARANCE SINCE THE PRIOR STUDY. THE LESION IN SEGMENT 4 WITH AN AIR-FLUID LEVEL IS LIKELY SURGICEL PACKING RATHER THAN ABSCESS. 2. SLIGHTLY INCREASED SIZE OF SMALL RIGHT POSTERIOR SUBPHRENIC FLUID COLLECTION WITH NEW MASS-EFFECT ON THE ADJACENT HEPATIC PARENCHYMA. TINY FOCI OF GAS WITHIN THIS FLUID COLLECTION MAY BE FROM ADJACENT SURGICEL INDICATING BILOMA; HOWEVER, DEVELOPING ABSCESS IS ALSO A POSSIBILITY. 3. DECREASED RIGHT PLEURAL EFFUSION WITH ASSOCIATED COMPRESSIVE ATELECTASIS, NOW TRACE IN AMOUNT. 4. THE REST OF THE FINDINGS ARE UNCHANGED FROM THE RECENT CT. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLY SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: Evaluation of the abdominal viscera is limited by the lack of intravenous contrast. There are increased bilateral small pleural effusions. There is associated increased bibasilar atelectasis and / or consolidation. Redemonstration of interlobular septal thickening likely representing mild pulmonary edema. Scattered atelectasis is present in the lingula and right middle lobe. The heart is mildly enlarged. There is a stable small to moderate pericardial effusion. The liver is enlarged, likely due to congestion. There is diffuse periportal edema. The gallbladder is distended and the gallbladder wall is thickened. Small amounts of high density material layers within the gallbladder which may represent vicarious excretion of contrast; unlikely to represent stones as no gallstones were seen on the same day ultrasound. No significant biliary ductal dilatation is seen. There is a subcentimeter intrinsically hyperdense lesion in the right kidney (4/156), suggestive of a hemorrhagic cyst. An additional subcentimeter low-attenuation lesion within the right kidney is too small to characterize but likely corresponds with the cyst seen on ultrasound (series 4, image 154). There is no hydronephrosis. The spleen is not visualized. Correlate with surgical history. Noncontrast appearance of the pancreas and adrenal glands is grossly unremarkable. The stomach, small bowel, and colon are normal in caliber. No abnormal bowel wall thickening is seen. The appendix appears normal and is filled with oral contrast. No free peritoneal air. There is marked diffuse mesenteric edema and a small volume of pelvic ascites. Small amount of retroperitoneal fluid is also present. There are numerous prominent retrocrural and small retroperitoneal lymph nodes. The largest retrocrural lymph node measures 13 mm in short axis. There is a left femoral arterial line. Urinary bladder is decompressed with a Foley catheter in place. Uterus is present. Mild degenerative changes are present in the thoracic spine. There is diffuse body wall edema. IMPRESSION: 1. GALLBLADDER WALL THICKENING AND MILD DISTENSION, AS WAS SEEN ON ULTRASOUND OF SAME DAY. IF THERE IS CLINICAL SUSPICION FOR ACALCULOUS ACUTE CHOLECYSTITIS, SHORT TERM FOLLOW-UP ULTRASOUND OR HIDA SCAN CAN BE CONSIDERED. 2. FINDINGS OF FLUID OVERLOAD WITH INCREASED SMALL BILATERAL PLEURAL EFFUSIONS, DIFFUSE PERIPORTAL AND MESENTERIC EDEMA, SMALL VOLUME ASCITES, RETROPERITONEAL FLUID AND BODY WALL EDEMA. 3. RETROCRURAL AND RETROPERITONEAL LYMPHADENOPATHY, NONSPECIFIC BUT POSSIBLY REACTIVE. RECOMMEND FOLLOW-UP IN 3 TO 6 MONTHS TO ENSURE RESOLUTION / STABILITY. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLY SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: A small hiatal hernia is noted about the distal esophagus and appear similar to previous examination in 4-26-2004. The liver appears decreased in attenuation, consistent with diffuse fatty infiltration. No focal liver lesion identified. The biliary tree, spleen, adrenal glands, and kidneys appear normal. Stable appearance of a cyst off the superior pole the right kidney. The urinary bladder and pelvic organs are unremarkable. Surgical clips are present in the gallbladder fossa consistent with prior cholecystectomy. Surgical mesh is again noted the anterior abdominal wall consistent with prior hernia repair. An 8 millimeter region of decreased attenuation is noted in the distal body of the pancreas and appears stable compared to prior examination in 4/1 / 2004 (3; 99 and 95). Increased soft tissue stranding is noted about the mesentery adjacent to a loop of distal small bowel within the mid pelvis (3; 301). The bowel wall of the ileum in this region also appears slightly thickened. No distinct mass is identified. No evidence of obstruction. The appendix is identified in the right lower quadrant and is normal. Diverticulosis of the sigmoid colon. Soft tissue prominence about the vaginal cuff is again noted. This appears slightly changed in confirmation, and shape, but overall is similar in size compared to previous examination and measures 2.8 x 2.6 cm (3; 350). No ascites is present. No suspicious osseous lesion identified. IMPRESSION: 1. INFLAMMATORY CHANGE AND MILD BOWEL WALL THICKENING IS NOTED ABOUT A SMALL SEGMENT OF THE DISTAL ILEUM WITHIN THE MID PELVIS. THIS IS NONSPECIFIC AND MAY REPRESENT A FOCAL INFECTIOUS OR INFLAMMATORY PROCESS. SHORT INTERVAL FOLLOW UP EXAMINATION IS RECOMMENDED TO ENSURE RESOLUTION. 2. DIVERTICULOSIS. 3. STABLE APPEARANCE OF A LOW ATTENUATION LESION WITHIN THE DISTAL BODY OF THE PANCREAS THAT LIKELY REPRESENTS A SIDE BRANCH IPMN. 4. AGAIN NOTED IS PROMINENCE OF SOFT TISSUE ABOUT THE VAGINAL CUFF. THIS IS STABLE COMPARED TO MOST RECENT COMPARISON EXAMINATION BUT INCREASED COMPARED TO A PRIOR STUDY IN 4/2 / 2003. IF CLINICAL CONCERN, FURTHER EVALUATION WITH ULTRASOUND IS RECOMMENDED. These findings were discussed by phone with KATIE ROBINSON MD at 1 p. m. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLY SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

related: mesentery adjacent to distal small bowel (location:present)

input: FINDINGS: The visualized lung bases are clear. There is a short segment of narrowing associated with wall thickening, hyperemia, mesenteric vessel engorgement, and marked surrounding inflammatory change involving the distal ileum beginning approximately 15 cm from the ileocecal valve. This results in mild dilation of a short segment of ileum proximally with evidence of stasis. Then proximal to this dilated segment is another short segment of relative narrowing. The entire distal ileum shows wall thickening. The more proximal small bowel loops are normal in caliber. Wall thickening of the nearby sigmoid colon likely represents extension of information rather than primary colitis. No loculated fluid collections or evidence of perforation identified. The spleen is enlarged. The liver is normal morphology, without focal lesion. The biliary tree, gallbladder, pancreas, adrenal glands, and kidneys appear normal. The urinary bladder is unremarkable. A partially calcified intramural myoma is identified in the posterior uterine wall. An exophytic subserosal left fundal fibroid is also noted. Bilateral ovaries appear physiologic. Multiple prominence presacral, pelvic and right lower quadrant lymph nodes are presumably reactive. The principal vascular structures of the abdomen and pelvis are patent. No suspicious osseous lesion identified. IMPRESSION: 1. FINDINGS COMPATIBLE WITH ACUTE CROHN'S FLARE MOST SIGNIFICANTLY INVOLVING THE DISTAL ILEUM, AS DESCRIBED. THERE IS MILD DILATION AND EVIDENCE OF STASIS OF A SHORT SEGMENT OF ILEUM PROXIMAL TO THIS AREA OF ACUTE INFLAMMATION AND STRICTURE. 2. ANOTHER SHORT SEGMENT OF RELATIVE NARROWING WITH MUCOSAL HYPEREMIA IS SEEN MORE PROXIMALLY IN THE DISTAL ILEUM, WHICH MAY BE A SUBACUTE OR CHRONIC STRICTURE GIVEN THE LACK OF SURROUNDING INFLAMMATORY CHANGE. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLY SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: VASCULAR: Within the imaged portion of the descending thoracic aorta, once again noted is a dissection flap, with a large posteriorly located false lumen and a thin anteriorly located true lumen. The dissection continues into the abdominal aorta. The celiac artery once again is noted to arise off both the true and false lumens. The dissection flap extends into the proximal celiac artery and terminates within the proximal portion of the celiac artery. The superior mesenteric artery also arises from both lumens, and the dissection flap once again extends distally into the superior mesenteric artery. The right renal artery arises from the collapsed true lumen (series 4 image 216), with mild narrowing at the origin. There is normal contrast opacification of the right renal artery. The left renal artery arises from the false lumen and is also normally opacified with contrast. Compared to the prior study, there is now contrast opacification of the infrarenal abdominal aorta, which was previously unopacified. There is an approximately 1.6 cm tear within the dissection flap (series 4 image 248) in the infrarenal abdominal aorta, compatible with recent fenestration. There is normal opacification of the aortobiiliac graft. The inferior mesenteric artery is normally opacified with contrast. The bilateral internal and external iliac arteries are unremarkable. The imaged portions of the common femoral arteries are within normal limits. Note is made of a low density fluid collection in the right inguinal region, measuring 6 x 4.5 cm, slightly larger compared to the prior study where it measured 5.4 x 3.5 cm. LUNG BASES: Interval development of bilateral pleural effusions right greater than left, with associated compressive atelectasis. Interval development of a small pericardial effusion. ABDOMEN: The liver, spleen, pancreas, and adrenal glands are unremarkable. The kidneys are symmetric in appearance, though mildly decreased in degree of enhancement, which may be related to timing. Bowel loops are unremarkable. PELVIS: The bladder, prostate, and rectum are within normal limits. There is a small amount of free fluid in the pelvis. Bone windows demonstrate no aggressive appearing lytic or blastic osseous lesions. IMPRESSION: 1. INTERVAL FENESTRATION OF A PREVIOUSLY DEMONSTRATED DISSECTION FLAP WITHIN THE ABDOMINAL AORTA, WITH PERFUSION OF A SEGMENT OF THE NATIVE ABDOMINAL AORTA AND THE AORTOBIILIAC GRAFT, THAT WAS NOT PERFUSED ON THE PRIOR STUDY. 2. NORMAL CONTRAST OPACIFICATION OF THE BILATERAL RENAL ARTERIES. PLEASE NOTE THAT THE RIGHT RENAL ARTERY ARISES FROM THE TRUE LUMEN, WHILE THE LEFT RENAL ARTERY ARISES FROM THE FALSE LUMEN. THERE IS SYMMETRIC, ALTHOUGH SOMEWHAT DECREASED ENHANCEMENT OF BOTH KIDNEYS, WHICH MAY BE RELATED TO TIMING OF IMAGE ACQUISITION. 3. INTERVAL DEVELOPMENT OF BILATERAL PLEURAL EFFUSIONS AND A SMALL PERICARDIAL EFFUSION. 4. MILD INTERVAL INCREASE IN SIZE OF A FLUID COLLECTION IN THE RIGHT INGUINAL REGION, WHICH MAY REPRESENT A POSTOPERATIVE HEMATOMA OR SEROMA. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDINGS, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

output.anchor: celiac artery arises from both lumens (observation:present)
superior mesenteric artery arises from both lumens (observation:present)
right renal artery arises from true lumen (observation:present)
left renal artery arises from false lumen (observation:present)

input: FINDINGS: Mild dilatation of multiple loops of proximal small bowel / jejunum, without clear transition point, with some of the loops of dilated bowel seen to have internal air-fluid levels (measuring up to 2.4 cm in luminal diameter). Findings are most consistent with intestinal ileus, although superimposed small bowel obstruction is not excluded. No abnormal intestinal wall thickening or hypoenhancement to suggest underlying bowel ischemia. No loculated fluid collections; mild ascites, principally perihepatic and tracking within the pelvis, most compatible with volume overload / cardiac dysfunction. Postsurgical changes status post right hemicolectomy with ileocolic anastomosis; no abnormal masses or stricturing at the anastomosis. Atrophic pancreas; nonspecific, small, 1.8 cm cystic lesion within head of pancreas (image 28, series 2), without solid features or abnormal enhancement, most compatible with a pancreatic cyst versus cystic pancreatic neoplasm, of doubtful clinical significance. Possible tiny, subcentimeter liver hemangioma, noted incidentally adjacent to gallbladder fossa; no suspicious liver masses or liver contour nodularity. Bilateral renal cortical thinning; scattered bilateral renal cysts, without hydronephrosis, nephrolithiasis, or solid renal masses. Biliary tract, gallbladder, spleen, and adrenals unremarkable. Urinary bladder mildly distended. Status post hysterectomy. Principal vessels patent and non-aneurysmal, on a background of moderate atheromatous changes of abdominal aorta and iliac arteries. No change in mild bilateral pleural effusions, right greater than left, with associated compressive atelectasis of the bilateral lower lobes. Heart size at upper limits of normal; no pericardial effusion. Severe streak beam-hardening artifact from left total hip arthroplasty, obscures anatomic detail within the pelvis. Multilevel degenerative changes of the thoracolumbar spine. Densely sclerotic lesions within the L2 and L4 vertebral bodies, most likely to represent bone islands / enostoses. IMPRESSION: 1. Mildly dilated loops of proximal small bowel, without clear transition point, most favored to represent an intestinal ileus versus, less likely, an early small bowel obstruction; no evidence of intestinal ischemia. 2. Postsurgical changes status post right hemicolectomy with ileocolic anastomosis. 3. Mild ascites and mild bilateral pleural effusions, most likely attributable to volume overload / underlying cardiac issues of the patient. Findings and impression discussed with Brylee Carson of emergency department at 1402 hours on June 13th, 2014 by DR REGINA of radiology. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: Images through the lower thorax demonstrate small bilateral, right greater left, pleural effusions. Patchy bilateral lower lobe airspace opacities are seen, superimposed on bilateral dependent atelectasis. The heart size is enlarged and pacing leads are seen within the right ventricle. Bone windows show diffuse osteopenia and mild discogenic degenerative changes of the thoracolumbar spine. Abdomen: There is a large hiatal hernia that contains the gastric fundus and portions of the gastric body along with the splenic flexure, new since the prior examination. Oral contrast is seen within the splenic flexure as well as within the left colon distal to the hiatal hernia. There is no proximal bowel obstruction. A large posterior right hepatic lobe simple cyst is seen. Additional subcentimeter hypodensities in liver are too small to adequately characterize but are stable since the prior enhanced CT examination and most likely represent hepatic cysts. Small amount of perihepatic fluid is seen. There is mild atrophy of the bilateral kidneys as well as the pancreas. Within the limits of an unenhanced study, the biliary tree, gallbladder, spleen, and right adrenal are within normal limits. There is mild diffuse thickening of left adrenal gland, stable and likely related to hyperplasia or adenomatous thickening. There is no free intraperitoneal air or fluid. No abdominal lymphadenopathy is seen. Moderate atherosclerotic calcification is seen within a normal sized abdominal aorta. Pelvis: The descending colon shows mural thickening and mild pericolonic stranding. Specifically, the segment of bowel involved includes the distal splenic flexure in the hernia to the sigmoid colon. Although sigmoid diverticula are seen, no significant diverticula involving the left colon are seen. There is no pneumatosis. The remainder the lower GI tract is within normal limits. The appendix is not well-visualized. The ureters and urinary bladder are within normal limits. The uterus is surgically absent. Small amount fluid is seen within the pelvis. There is no pelvic lymphadenopathy. IMPRESSION: 1. LONG SEGMENT MURAL THICKENING WITH MILD PERICOLONIC STRANDING INVOLVING THE DESCENDING COLON. DIFFERENTIAL CONSIDERATIONS INCLUDE ISCHEMIC COLITIS, ESPECIALLY GIVEN UNDERLYING HEART DISEASE. INFECTIOUS COLITIS IS AN ALTERNATIVE CONSIDERATION. 2. LARGE HIATAL HERNIA CONTAINING STOMACH AND SPLENIC FLEXURE. NO PROXIMAL BOWEL DILATATION TO SUGGEST OBSTRUCTION 3. NONSPECIFIC BILATERAL LOWER LOBE PATCHY AIRSPACE OPACITIES, REPRESENTING ATELECTASIS VERSUS PNEUMONIA. 4. SIGMA DIVERTICULOSIS WITHOUT EVIDENCE FOR DIVERTICULITIS. THESE FINDINGS WERE DISCUSSED WITH Jefferson Bradley Alexander BY Castro Braylon AT 0340 HOURS ON 8/18/2016. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLY SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: Limited visualization of the lower thorax demonstrates normal heart size. There is a central line, which terminates in the right atrium. Motion artifact limits evaluation, however, no large focal lung parenchymal abnormality is appreicated. There are two subcentimeter hypodense lesions within the left lobe of the liver, too small to characterize, stable from prior. There is minimal central biliary dilatation in the right lobe. A trace amount of ascites is seen at the inferior aspect of the right lobe. This is new from prior. The gallbladder is unremarkable. There are no radiopaque gallstones. The common bile duct appears normal in caliber. Redemonstration of a stable low density lesion within the body of the pancreas measuring 1.0 cm without associated ductal dilatation. The spleen demonstrates interval enlargement of a round hypodense lesion, compatible with metastasis, now measuring 3.2 x 2.8 cm, previously 2.8 x 2.1 cm. Additionally, there is new small volume perisplenic ascites. There is stable nodularity of the right adrenal gland, which may reflect small metastases. The left adrenal gland is normal. The stomach is not distended. There is an enteric tube, with tip in the fundus. There are ill-defined low-density regions along the greater curvature, grossly stable from prior, which may correspond to the FDG avid areas on recent PET scan 3-13-2017, however, are poorly evaluted due to lack of luminal distention The duodenum is decompressed. The kidneys demonstrate symmetric enhancement and excretion without evidence of hydronephrosis. There is mild perinephric stranding bilaterally, unchanged from prior. There are multiple very small hypodense lesions seen in the renal cortex, too small to characterize. A larger 2.4 cm cystic lesion is seen in the right kidney, unchanged from prior. The small bowel appears normal in caliber with uniform enhancement. The large bowel is also normal in caliber. Sigmoid diverticula are seen without inflammatory changes to suggest diverticulitis. There is a stable fat-containing right inguinal hernia. The aorta, IVC, and portovenous system appear patent. There is a 1.6 cm fusiform aneurysm of the celiac trunk. There are two right-sided renal arteries. General survey for metastases demonstrates a 5 x 5 cm heterogeneously enhancing lobulated gastrohepatic ligament mass, with areas of necrosis. This is increased in size compared to prior, which measured approximately 3.7 cm transversely. There are multiple adjacent satellite nodules, which have also increased in size. There is an interval increase in size and fusion of two gastrosplenic ligament nodules, which now measure 2.4 and 1.8 cm, previously 1.7 and 1.2 cm. There is an interval increase in size of two nodules along the greater curvature of the stomach in the omentum, both measuring over 1 cm. There is a metastasis in the right paracolic gutter, measuring 13 mm, previously 11 mm. Additionally, there are the splenic and adrenal lesions described earlier. Regional osseous structures demonstrate mild multi-level degenerative changes. There is a trace amount of new pelvic ascites. IMPRESSION: 1. INTERVAL INCREASE IN SIZE OF KNOWN METASTATIC LESIONS AS DESCRIBED ABOVE. THE LARGEST LESIONS INVOLVE THE GASTROHEPATIC LIGAMENT, THE GASTROSPLENIC LIGAMENT, THE SPLEEN, THE OMENTUM AT THE GREATER CURVATURE OF THE STOMACH, AND POSSIBLY THE GASTRIC WALL ITSELF. NO ASSOCIATED OBSTRUCTION / DILATATION OF THE STOMACH OR BOWEL. 2.1. 6 CM CELIAC TRUNK ANEURYSM, STABLE FROM PRIOR. CONSIDER FURTHER EVALUATION WITH CT ANGIOGRAM OF THE ABDOMEN AND PELVIS. 3. MULTIPLE SIGMOID DIVERTICULA, WITH NO EVIDENCE OF DIVERTICULITIS. 4. NEW TRACE PERISPLENIC, PERIHEPATIC, AND PELVIC ASCITES AS DESCRIBED ABOVE. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDINGS, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

input: FINDINGS: The lung bases are unremarkable. The heart is normal in size without evidence of pericardial effusion. A 6-mm low-attenuation lesion is seen in segment 5 of the liver (image 8, series 7), which is too small to characterize. The liver is otherwise normal in attenuation. The portal veins and hepatic veins are patent. The gallbladder is unremarkable. There is no intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation. The spleen is normal in size and attenuation. Both adrenal glands are normal in morphology. The pancreas is unremarkable without evidence of pancreatic ductal dilatation. A 5-mm low-attenuation lesion is seen at the midpole of the left kidney (image 92, series 7), which is too small to further characterize. At the mid pole of the right kidney, there is a 2.3 x 2.7 cm mildly infiltrative cortically-based enhancing lesion, which shows several foci of calcification on noncontrast imaging. This lesion extends to the renal pelvis. Findings are highly concerning for malignancy. The right renal vein is patent. There is no retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy. The ureters are not well opacified; however, there is no evidence of hydronephrosis. Small bowel loops are normal in caliber. The large bowel is unremarkable. A normal air-filled appendix is seen in the right lower quadrant. An irregular 2.4 x 3.4 cm mass is seen at the left posterior lateral aspect of the urinary bladder (image 341, series 7), worrisome for carcinoma. This mass contains punctate foci of calcification. The prostate gland and seminal vesicles are unremarkable. There is no pelvic lymphadenopathy. The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber with mild atherosclerotic calcification. The imaged osseous structures are unremarkable. IMPRESSION: 1. IRREGULAR ENHANCING LESION AT THE LEFT POSTERIOR LATERAL ASPECT OF THE URINARY BLADDER, MEASURING 2.4 X 3.4 CM, CONCERNING FOR UROTHELIAL CARCINOMA. CORRELATION WITH CYSTOSCOPY IS RECOMMENDED. 2. ENHANCING LESION WITH PUNCTATE FOCI OF CALCIFICATION IN THE MIDPOLE OF THE RIGHT KIDNEY, MEASURING 2.3 X 2.7 CM, WHICH MAY REPRESENT RENAL CELL CARCINOMA OR POSSIBLY UROTHELIAL CARCINOMA GIVEN BLADDER FINDINGS DESCRIBED ABOVE. NO EVIDENCE OF RENAL VEIN THROMBOSIS OR RETROPERITONEAL LYMPHADENOPATHY. SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

There is no perihepatic hematoma. There is no significant intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary dilatation, with the common duct at the level of the porta hepatis measuring 7 mm. The gallbladder is surgically absent. The intrahepatic IVC and hepatic veins are patent. The portal veins are patent and normal in size. The splenic vein and SMV are widely patent. The spleen is normal in size and appearance, without evidence of a splenic laceration or adjacent hematoma. The pancreas is normal in appearance, without evidence of a pancreatic mass, ductal dilatation, or peripancreatic free fluid. Both adrenal glands are normal, without a discrete nodule or mass. The left kidney is normal in appearance, without evidence of hydronephrosis, stone, or renal laceration. There is normal perfusion of the left kidney on the delayed images, with normal excretion of contrast into the left collecting system. As before, patient is status post coiling of a right renal pseudoaneurysm (4/136). The right kidney is normal in appearance, without evidence of hydronephrosis, stone, or a renal laceration. There is normal perfusion of the right kidney on the delayed images, with normal excretion of contrast into the right collecting system. The stomach and duodenum are in normal anatomic positions. Small bowel loops are normal in caliber, without evidence of dilatation or wall thickening. There is no adjacent mesenteric stranding or hematoma to suggest mesenteric injury. There are scattered diverticula in the left colon, without evidence of diverticulitis. The remainder of the colon is normal in appearance, without evidence of wall thickening, pneumatosis, or pericolonic fat stranding. A normal appendix is identified in the right lower quadrant (4/236). The bladder is normal in appearance, without evidence of wall thickening, mass, or stone. The uterus is unremarkable, with a intrauterine contraceptive device (IUD) in place. The adnexa are unremarkable. There is no free fluid, loculated fluid collection, or abscess. There is no abnormal inflammation or hematoma in the abdomen or pelvis. There are no enlarged lymph nodes in the abdomen or pelvis. The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber, with a patent celiac, SMA, and IMA. There is a single renal artery bilaterally, with conventional renal vein anatomy. There is no evidence of a significant arterial vascular stenosis or traumatic vascular injury. As before, there is a wide neck, fat-containing left lateral lumbar hernia (4/192). BONES: There is no acute fracture. Spinal alignment is normal. There is no significant degeneration of the spine. There is no suspicious lytic or sclerotic bone lesion. IMPRESSION: 1. NO EVIDENCE OF TRAUMATIC INJURY IN THE ABDOMEN OR PELVIS. 2. STATUS POST COILING OF A RIGHT RENAL PSEUDOANEURYSM SUMMARY: 2-ABNORMAL, PREVIOUSLY REPORTED I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.

The largest perforator of the right inferior epigastric artery (series 5, image 348) exits into the subcutaneous fat at the level of the umbilicus on series 5, image 311. The largest perforator of the left inferior epigastric artery also exits into the subcutaneous fat at the level of the umbilicus (series 5, image 266). Visualized portions of the heart are normal in appearance and size. No pericardial effusion ABDOMEN: Evaluation of the abdominal solid organs is suboptimal due to the arterial phase of imaging. The liver, gallbladder, pancreas, spleen, adrenal glands, and kidneys are normal in appearance. The small and large bowel are normal in course and caliber. Dense clips are present in the right lower quadrant of the abdomen (series 5, image 331) The bladder, uterus, and adnexa appear unremarkable. No abdominal, pelvic, or inguinal lymphadenopathy identified. MUSCULOSKELETAL: Evaluation of the osseous structures demonstrates no suspicious appearing lesions. Bilateral breast implants are partially visualized. IMPRESSION: 1. PERFORATING SUBCUTANEOUS VESSELS OF THE BILATERAL INFERIOR EPIGASTRIC ARTERIES ARE LOCATED AT THE LEVEL OF THE UMBILICUS. DETAILED MEASUREMENTS AND LOCALIZATION WILL BE PROVIDED BY THE 3-D LABORATORY. SUMMARY: 1-NO SIGNIFICANT ABNORMALITY I have personally reviewed the images for this examination and agreed with the report transcribed above.
